title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard introduced by Google that enables AI agents to interact with websites through structured tools instead of DOM parsing and pixel clicking. It provides both declarative HTML-based and imperative JavaScript-based APIs for registering actions, currently in early preview on Chrome 146.
writeup: This talk from Google's Tara Agyemang introduces WebMCP, a proposed web standard that lets AI agents act on websites through structured tools rather than DOM parsing and pixel-level clicking. The standard offers two ways to register actions: a declarative HTML-based API and an imperative JavaScript-based API. It currently runs in early preview on Chrome 146. The shift matters because it abstracts away the DOM, giving agents a more reliable interface for browser automation and real-time interaction.
